issues.title: Remove node modules folder from source code

### Context :
Present source code contains node modules in the root directory. 
Node modules are required for running the app locally but since could be installed via one script run, thay shouldn't be pushed to github repo.
___

- Delete the node modules folder.
- Raise a PR.

Create Dashboard Section for Approver
Create a Bug Template
issues.body:
Create `bug_template.md` in `.github` folder

This template will be futher used for reporting bugs in future.
The Expectation is to create a template which need not to be modified again and again.

issues.title: Create PR Template
issues.body:
create `PR_template` in `.github` folder.

This template will be futher used for reporting PRs in future so that the reviewer could have enough context.
The expectation is to create a template which need not to be modified again and again.

issues.title: Create a Issue Template
issues.body:
Use the `.github` folder and create a `issue_template.md`.

This template will be futher used for creating issue description for future. We use issue template so that the person assigned the issue is able to understand what is the context and expectations from issue.
The Expectation is to create a template which need not to be modified again and again.

issues.title: PyTorch Installation Issue on M1, M2, and M3 Macs + Need for Troubleshooting Guide
issues.body:
While setting up the repository on Macs with M1, M2, and M3 chips, I encountered issues installing PyTorch. Although the README.md includes setup instructions, it lacks troubleshooting information for Macs with M-series chips, particularly regarding PyTorch installation. To help new contributors, I suggest adding a section to the README.md or creating a separate troubleshooting guide (e.g., TROUBLESHOOTING.md) with instructions for:

1. PyTorch installation issues on M1, M2, and M3 Macs.
2. Platform-specific tips and workarounds.
3. Common error resolutions for M-series chips.

> This would help streamline the process for new contributors using Macs with M-series chips.

**Proposed Solution:**

- Add troubleshooting information for PyTorch installation on M1, M2, and M3 Macs to the README.md or a new TROUBLESHOOTING.md file.

- Include tips and workarounds for common issues faced on these Macs.

Tagging Relevant People:
@prarabdhshukla – Requesting guidance on handling this issue and feedback on adding the troubleshooting guide.

issues.title: Documentation Update And Manage.py To Ease Superuser creation
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**

After setting up the development server and headed towards creating superuser, I ran the following command 

`python manage.py runserver`

I recieved : 

`Couldn't import Django. Are you sure it's installed and 
available on your PYTHONPATH environment variable? Did you 
forget to activate a virtual environment?`

**Inorder to ease The process of Installation for First-time-contributers, following should be done:**

- The Documentation should be updated to tell about how to get in docker container and then create superuser.
- The Message in manage.py should be updated to say that there is a need to get in docker container and then create a superuser.


**Additional context**
Add any other context or screenshots about the feature request here.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a5cf4987-0fe1-4047-bcd9-21e201c8c72e)

issues.title: Models for handling Blog Data
issues.body:
hii there,

Currently there is no model and view functions to store Blog data and hence every blog is to be hard coded. I will Create models which will store data about blogpost including,

- Title of Blogpost
- Content of Blogpost
- Owner Details

Another Model for storing comments corresponding to each blogpost. this model will contain the details of,

-Person who Comments
-Time of Comment
-Contents of Comment

kindly Assign me this issue and also add tags of hacktobfest and hacktoberfest-accepted.

regards 
Anurag

issues.title: Missing Requirements.txt and Shifting Back The Database to Sqlite
issues.body:
There are multiple modules like widget-tewaks and django-filter which is being used and not mentioned in requirements.txt which creates frustration while running the server.

Also, The database should be SQLite not mySQL for development purposes.

Kindly assign me this issue and add hacktoberfest-accepted and hacktoberfest2024 tags. I will fix the above issues and also leave the database config untouched( commenting the code), so that when the development is completed it becomes easy for deployment.

Also while searching for Django repos in which I could Contribute, I have never found such a clean and well written code, feels like you are a very compassionate coder. Kudos to you code.

Regards 
Anurag

issues.title: I want to add a Signup and Login Page
issues.body:
There is a need of a page using which user may signin or signup, following are the things which should be carried out.

- Signup and Signin Templates.
- Url Mapping of templates
- views that will handle the Authentication request
- finally register the user to the admin panel
